rationale: The postcondition requires structural equality post1_self_ == post2_self_ for StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, which contains exec Vec fields (keys.v: Vec<K>, vals: Vec<ID>). In this Verus version, Vec<T> has no view-injectivity axiom (no v1@ == v2@ ==> v1 == v2) and no #[verifier::ext_equal], while valid()/view() on the map are closed. We derive post1_self_@ == post2_self_@ (Map ext-equal) and pointwise gap equality, but bridging to struct equality requires an axiom that isn't available, and we are forbidden from introducing assume/external_body/assume_specification. Tried direct assertion of ==, =~=, and =~~=; only the view/gap equalities discharge. Confirms previous attempt's analysis.
